title: Vessel N°1
description: A hand-thrown stoneware vessel with an organic form inspired by natural erosion patterns. The surface features a matte glaze with subtle variations in tone, creating a sense of depth and movement.

title: Vessel N°2
description: A sculptural ceramic piece exploring the boundaries between functional pottery and abstract art. Its asymmetric silhouette draws from geological formations.

title: Erosion Bowl
description: A wide, shallow bowl with deliberately rough edges that suggest natural weathering. The interior is smooth and glazed, contrasting with the raw exterior.

title: Fragment Series I
description: Part of an ongoing series examining the beauty of broken and reconstructed forms. Each fragment is individually fired and reassembled with visible joins.

title: Tea Set — Quiet Morning
description: A minimal tea set consisting of a teapot and four cups. The soft, rounded forms and warm earth tones evoke a sense of calm and ritual.

title: Tall Vase — Stratum
description: A tall, narrow vase with horizontal texture lines reminiscent of geological strata. The surface shifts between matte and semi-gloss finishes.
